title: API Token
user_story:
As a webmaster\
I want to restrict certain API endpoints\
So not everyone can randomly create or delete data
description:
* POST and DELETE requests always require authentication
* for GET requests authentication is not needed
  * the API token is ignored when the user sends it anyway
  * (in a later iteration it might be used for calculating the user’s rate limit)
* UPDATE requests do not exist yet
  * the API token should be implemented in such a way that it is easy to extend later
* in this first iteration the API token does not need to be user-specific → it can be a shared “password“, like the basic auth credentials
* the implementation should follow best practices regarding
  * the interface → to avoid breaking changes in the API specification in the following iterations
  * security → to avoid being vulnerable to simple attack vectors
